info.name: insurance-enterprise-v1-stable
info.description:
Enterprise API Automation Framework for Insurance PAS

Includes:

- Setup layer for data initialization
    
- Functional lifecycle testing (Quote flow)
    
- Negative validation (400/404/405 scenarios)
    
- Contract testing using JSON schema validation
    
- Performance testing with SLA thresholds
    
- Multi-environment support (DEV/QA/PROD)
    
- CI integration with Newman + GitHub Actions

item.name: 5.1 [NEGATIVE] Wrong HTTP Method on Status Endpoint (Expect 405)

exec: // Using PUT instead of PATCH on /quotes/{id}/status
// Backend only supports PATCH - PUT should return 405 Method Not Allowed
pm.test("Status code is 405 Method Not Allowed (wrong HTTP method)", function () {

request.description: Verifies that using the wrong HTTP method (PUT instead of PATCH) on the status endpoint returns 405 Method Not Allowed. The backend only exposes PATCH /quotes/{id}/status.

item.name: 5.2 [NEGATIVE] Illegal Backward State Transition ISSUED → SUBMITTED (Expect 400)

exec: // After the happy path, the quote is in ISSUED (final) state.
// Attempting to transition ISSUED → SUBMITTED is an illegal backward move.
pm.test("Status code is 400 for illegal backward state transition", function () {
pm.test("Error response contains transition-related message", function () {
}, 'Expected error message about invalid state transition');

request.description: Verifies that the state machine rejects illegal backward transitions. After the happy path, the quote is ISSUED (locked final state). Attempting ISSUED → SUBMITTED must return 400 with a descriptive error.

item.name: 4.1 [RIDER] Create Fresh Quote for RIDER Test

exec: pm.test("Fresh quote created in DRAFT for RIDER scenario", function () {

request.description: Creates a fresh DRAFT quote for the RIDER cover scenario using the same agent and client from the happy path setup.

exec: pm.test("Insured added successfully for RIDER scenario", function () {

request.description: Adds a PRIMARY insured person to the RIDER test quote.

exec: pm.test("BASE cover (LC) created as parent for RIDER", function () {

request.description: Creates the BASE (LC) cover which the RIDER will attach to as its parent. The coverId from this response is stored as riderBaseCoverId.

item.name: 4.4 [RIDER] Add RIDER Cover (WOP) - Attached to BASE

exec: pm.test("RIDER cover (WOP) created and linked to BASE cover", function () {
console.log("✅ RIDER cover attached to parentCoverId:", json.parentCoverId);

request.description: Creates a RIDER cover (WOP - Waiver of Premium) attached to the BASE (LC) cover via parentCoverId. Tests the RIDER code path in CoverValidatorEngine which was previously completely untested. The RIDER must reference a valid BASE cover on the same quote and same insured.

item.name: 4.5 [RIDER] GET Cover Tree - Verify BASE + RIDER Hierarchy

exec: pm.test("Cover tree contains BASE cover with RIDER attached", function () {
// BASE node should have the RIDER as a child
console.log("✅ RIDER cover hierarchy verified in cover tree");

request.description: Uses the cover-tree endpoint (GET /quotes/{id}/cover-tree) to verify the BASE→RIDER parent-child hierarchy is correctly stored. This is the only way to confirm the parentCoverId relationship is persisted and returned in tree form.

exec: // A RIDER cover MUST have a parentCoverId.
// Omitting it should return 400 Bad Request.
pm.test("Status code is 400 for RIDER without parentCoverId", function () {
pm.test("Error message references RIDER or parentCoverId requirement", function () {
'Expected error about missing parentCoverId for RIDER'

request.description: Negative test: Attempts to create a RIDER cover without the mandatory parentCoverId field. The backend CoverValidatorEngine should reject this with 400 since RIDER covers must reference a parent BASE cover.

exec: pm.test("BOOLEAN attribute created with correct fields", function () {

request.description: Tests the BOOLEAN valueType for cover attributes. Only booleanValue must be set (stringValue and numberValue must be null/absent). This is the only BOOLEAN type test in the entire suite — previously STRING and NUMBER were the only types exercised.

item.name: 4.8 [ATTRIBUTE] NEGATIVE - BOOLEAN Type with stringValue Instead (400)

exec: // Backend rule: exactly one of stringValue/numberValue/booleanValue must be set,
// matching the declared valueType. Sending stringValue when valueType=BOOLEAN is invalid.
pm.test("Status code is 400 for BOOLEAN type with wrong value field", function () {
'Expected error about wrong value field for BOOLEAN valueType'

request.description: Negative test: valueType is BOOLEAN but stringValue is provided instead of booleanValue. The backend strictly validates that exactly one value field matches the declared valueType. Expects 400.

exec: // Backend rule: at least one of stringValue/numberValue/booleanValue must be set.
// Sending only attributeKey + valueType with no value is invalid.
pm.test("Status code is 400 when no value field is provided", function () {

request.description: Negative test: No value field (stringValue/numberValue/booleanValue) is provided. The backend should reject with 400 since the attribute has no actual value to store.

item.name: 5.5 [MULTI-INSURED] GET Insured Persons - Verify All 3 Roles

exec: pm.test("All 3 insured persons are present with correct roles", function () {
pm.test("Exactly one insured has isPrimary=true", function () {
pm.test("Each insured has a unique insuredId", function () {
console.log("✅ Multi-insured scenario verified - 3 persons with distinct roles");

request.description: Retrieves all insured persons for the quote and verifies: (1) all 3 roles present, (2) exactly one isPrimary=true on the PRIMARY person, (3) all insuredIds are unique.

exec: // The backend must enforce that only one insured can have isPrimary=true.
// Attempting to add a second PRIMARY with isPrimary=true should return 400.
pm.test("Status code is 400 for duplicate PRIMARY insured", function () {
pm.test("Error references primary or duplicate insured constraint", function () {
'Expected error about duplicate PRIMARY insured constraint'
console.log("✅ Duplicate PRIMARY insured correctly rejected");

request.description: Negative test: Attempts to add a second insured with role=PRIMARY and isPrimary=true on a quote that already has a PRIMARY insured. The backend must reject this to enforce the single-primary-per-quote business rule.

exec: // An ISSUED quote is a terminal state — no new covers can be added.
// Backend should reject with 400 (invalid state) or 409 (conflict).
pm.test("Status code is 400 or 409 — ISSUED quote rejects new covers", function () {
pm.test("Error references ISSUED state or immutability", function () {
'Expected error about mutation on ISSUED quote'
console.log("✅ ISSUED quote correctly rejected cover addition");

request.description: Attempts to add a new cover to an ISSUED quote. The backend state machine must reject all mutations on ISSUED quotes. Expects 400 or 409.

item.name: 6.2 [IMMUTABILITY] Add Insured Person to ISSUED Quote (Expect 400/409)

exec: pm.test("Status code is 400 or 409 — ISSUED quote rejects new insured persons", function () {
pm.test("Error references ISSUED state or mutation rejection", function () {
'Expected error about mutation on ISSUED quote'
console.log("✅ ISSUED quote correctly rejected insured person addition");

request.description: Attempts to add a new insured person to an ISSUED quote. Expects 400 or 409 since ISSUED is a terminal, locked state.

item.name: 6.3 [IMMUTABILITY] Add Attribute to Cover on ISSUED Quote (Expect 400/409)

exec: // Covers on an ISSUED quote should also be locked.
// Adding a new attribute to a cover that belongs to an ISSUED quote must be rejected.
pm.test("Status code is 400 or 409 — cover on ISSUED quote rejects new attributes", function () {
pm.test("Error references ISSUED or locked state", function () {
'Expected error about mutation on cover belonging to ISSUED quote'
console.log("✅ Cover on ISSUED quote correctly rejected attribute addition");

request.description: Attempts to add a new attribute to a cover that belongs to an ISSUED quote. Covers inherit the locked state from their parent quote. Expects 400 or 409.

exec: // PUT /covers/{id} to update financial fields should also be locked on ISSUED quotes.
pm.test("Status code is 400 or 409 — cover update rejected on ISSUED quote", function () {
pm.test("Error references ISSUED or locked state", function () {
'Expected error about updating cover on ISSUED quote'
console.log("✅ Cover update on ISSUED quote correctly rejected");

request.description: Attempts to update financial fields (sumAssured, benefitAmount, termYears) on a cover that belongs to an ISSUED quote. This mutation must be rejected since ISSUED quotes are fully locked. Expects 400 or 409.

exec: // ISSUED is the terminal state. Any backward or lateral transition must be rejected.
// Attempting ISSUED → APPROVED (backward step) should return 400.
pm.test("Status code is 400 for backward transition from ISSUED", function () {
pm.test("Error references invalid transition or ISSUED state", function () {
'Expected error about invalid backward state transition from ISSUED'
console.log("✅ Backward transition from ISSUED correctly rejected");

request.description: Attempts ISSUED → APPROVED (backward transition). The state machine enforces DRAFT→SUBMITTED→APPROVED→ISSUED as a one-way flow. Any backward or lateral transition from ISSUED must be rejected with 400.

// ─── GLOBAL SLA: maxResponseTime check (applies to every request) ───────────
// Skips SLA check on intentional negative tests (4xx expected responses).
// For those, performance is less meaningful than correctness.
// ─── GLOBAL FAILURE LOGGER (logs full request + response on any 4xx/5xx) ───
console.log('ℹ️ Expected error response for:', pm.info.requestName, '→ HTTP', pm.response.code);
